note_evidence: Monica Hilpert is a 76-year-old woman admitted to skilled nursing facility care today, transferred from the acute hospital after an inpatient stay of nearly a week, for continued nursing care and rehabilitation
rationale: Age, SNF admission, ~1 week hospitalization, and rehab all documented.

note_evidence: profound fatigue and deconditioning — by the end of the hospitalization she could not manage short household distances on her feet
rationale: Deconditioning with inability to ambulate documented.

note_evidence: History includes osteoporosis
hyperlipidemia
prediabetes
extensive tooth loss; she chews on one side only, and her meals have contracted over the years toward small, soft foods
rationale: Tooth loss, one-sided chewing, soft foods documented.

note_evidence: a deep, aching low back pain radiating into both hips, worst with prolonged standing
rationale: Location, radiation, aggravation, and quality all documented.

note_evidence: disturbs sleep on bad nights
worst with prolonged standing ... Stands only with support and a fixed target ... guarded, slow position changes
rationale: Standing worsens pain, slow position changes, needs support to stand.

note_evidence: Started naproxen sodium 220 mg tablets for baseline pain control
rationale: Naproxen 220 mg for baseline pain documented.

note_evidence: Started meperidine hydrochloride 50 mg tablets for more severe pain, to be used sparingly
rationale: Meperidine 50 mg PRN for severe pain documented.

note_evidence: close nursing observation for sedation, confusion, or unsteadiness given her age
rationale: Opioid risks in elderly and monitoring counseled.

note_evidence: She lives alone with minimal social contact; her sister died two years ago, and a neighbor provides her only regular help
rationale: Lives alone, sister died, neighbor help documented.

note_evidence: She discloses a remote abusive marriage that she left; the former partner has no current presence in her life and she reports no present safety concerns
rationale: Safety screen negative for current threat documented.

note_evidence: her meals have contracted over the years toward small, soft foods, raising concern for longstanding under-nutrition
rationale: Chronic undernutrition from poor dentition documented.

note_evidence: Social work to arrange home supports before discharge and to connect her with community or senior-center programs
Dietitian consultation for texture-modified, protein- and calcium-forward meals balanced for glucose and lipids
rationale: Dietitian referral with diet details documented.

note_evidence: Physical therapy for safe transfers, standing tolerance, and gait strengthening
Occupational therapy sessions for dressing, bathing safety, and daily tasks
History and physical examination and the initial patient assessment were completed today
the initial patient assessment were completed today
Development of individualized plan of care with the interdisciplinary team
Daily nursing care and supplementary surveillance every shift (pain, vital signs, skin, medications)
rationale: Nursing surveillance with monitored items documented.

note_evidence: Strict call-before-standing instruction reinforced; call bell education provided and accepted
rationale: Fall precautions and call button counseling documented.

note_evidence: Formal pre-discharge assessment of mobility, medication and meal management, and home setup prior to release
Anticipate a stay of roughly two weeks depending on functional progress
regimen to be changed if poorly tolerated
transferred from the acute hospital after an inpatient stay of nearly a week
History and physical examination and the initial patient assessment were completed today
